methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
Enapter is a public-stage Climate & Energy company. The valuation landscape includes a sparse peer universe: one public comparable (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and eight private peers at various stages, including QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion. The Climate & Energy sector contains 21 same-sector peers broadly, but strict stage and tier filters yield insufficient depth for reliable peer-median triangulation.

Standard peer-median methodology is not applicable here. The same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier screens produced fewer than three datapoints in each category, falling short of the minimum threshold needed for robust multiples analysis. A broad all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the capital intensity and long development cycles characteristic of climate technology versus faster-cycling software or services comparables.

Without sufficient narrow-peer density to support multiples-based triangulation, valuation synthesis relies on qualitative peer positioning rather than quantified trading or exit multiples. Key uncertainties include the absence of consensus comps at Enapter's specific stage, the heterogeneity of capital efficiency and commercialization timelines across the peer set, and reliance on the single public marker (LNZA) as an imperfect proxy for valuation sentiment in this subsector. Any valuation range derived would require supplemental methods—cost-of-capital, precedent transaction, or management guidance—to achieve defensibility.
